title: Lentzea indica sp. nov., a novel actinobacteria isolated from Indian Himalayan-soil.
abstract: A novel actinobacterium, strain PSKA42<sup>T</sup> was isolated from a soil sample of Kashmir Himalaya, India (latitude: 34°-01' N; longitude: 74°-47' E; altitude: 5328 ft). The morphological, biochemical and molecular characteristics predicted that PSKA42<sup>T</sup> belongs to the genera Lentzea. Using the Ezbiocloud server it has also been manifested that the 16S rRNA gene sequence of PSKA42 was 98.4% similar with Lentzea guizhouensis DHSC013<sup>T</sup>, Lentzea albidocapillata subsp. violacea IMSNU50388<sup>T</sup>; 98.26% similar with Lentzea aerocolonigenes NRRLB-3298<sup>T</sup>, Lentzea albidocapillata subsp. albidocapillata NRRLB-24057<sup>T</sup> and 98.19% similar with Lentzea californiensis DSM43393<sup>T</sup>. Whole-genome average nucleotide identity values between PSKA42<sup>T</sup> and L. guizhouensis DHSC013<sup>T</sup>, L. albidocapillata subsp. violaera IMSNU50388<sup>T</sup>, L. aerocolonigenes NRRLB-3298<sup>T</sup>, L. albidocapillata subsp. albidocapillata NRRLB-24057<sup>T</sup>, L. californiensis DSM43393<sup>T</sup>, L. flaviverucosa As40578<sup>T</sup> were 84.94%, 85.8%, 87.15%, 87.71%, 79.29, and 87.22%, respectively, suggesting that PSKA42<sup>T</sup> represented a new species. In-silco genome-to-genome distances analysis and MLSA with selected housekeeping genes also indicated that the isolate should be assigned to a new species under the genus Lentzea. Analysis of the whole-cell hydrolysate found mannose and ribose as major sugar while cell wall amino acid was identified as meso-diaminopimelic acid. Major fatty acids (> 5%) were observed as 15:0 iso (23.81%), and 16:0 iso (26.11%). PSKA42 contains polyketide synthase type-I genes but unable to show antimicrobial activity in laboratory conditions. The average GC content in its genome is 68.3%. Further, based on physicochemical, chemotaxonomic markers and molecular analysis, PSKA42<sup>T</sup> can be assigned as novel species of Lentzea, we proposed Lentzea indica sp nov. The type strain is PSKA42<sup>T</sup> (JCM 33729<sup>T</sup>, MTCC 12936<sup>T</sup>, MCC 4127<sup>T</sup>).
